name: Britney Spears
description:
'''Britney Jean Spears''' (born December 2, 1981) is an American singer. Referred to as the "[[Honorific nicknames in popular music|Princess of Pop]]", she is widely regarded as [[Cultural impact of Britney Spears|one of the most culturally significant figures of the 21st century]]. She is known for her distinctive vocal style, energetic stage performances, and contributions to the revival of [[teen pop]] music.

Spears began her career in the early 1990s as a cast member of the musical ''[[Ruthless!]]'' and the television show ''[[The All-New Mickey Mouse Club]]''. After signing with [[Jive Records]] in 1997, she rose to fame with her debut single, "[[...Baby One More Time]]", which reached number-one on the US [[Billboard Hot 100|''Billboard'' Hot 100]] and became one of the [[List of best-selling singles|best-selling singles of all time]]. Her first three studio albums—''[[...Baby One More Time (album)|...Baby One More Time]]'' (1999), ''[[Oops!... I Did It Again (album)|Oops!... I Did It Again]]'' (2000), and ''[[Britney (album)|Britney]]'' (2001)—each topped the US [[Billboard 200|''Billboard'' 200]], with the former two ranking among  the [[List of best-selling albums|best-selling albums of all time]]. She made her film debut in ''[[Longshot (film)|Longshot]]'' (2001) before co-starring in ''[[Crossroads (2002 film)|Crossroads]]'' the following year. Her next two studio albums, ''[[In the Zone]]'' (2003) and ''[[Blackout (Britney Spears album)|Blackout]]'' (2007), were influenced by [[hip-hop]], [[dance music|dance]], and [[electronic music|electronic]] genres. The former included the single "[[Toxic (song)|Toxic]]", which won the [[Grammy Award for Best Dance/Electronic Recording]].

Amid years of public scrutiny and [[mental health]] challenges, Spears was placed under a [[conservatorship]] in 2008. That same year, she released her sixth studio album, ''[[Circus (Britney Spears album)|Circus]]'', which was led by her second US number-one single, "[[Womanizer (song)|Womanizer]]". Over the next several years, she released the studio albums ''[[Femme Fatale (Britney Spears album)|Femme Fatale]]'' (2011), ''[[Britney Jean]]'' (2013), and ''[[Glory (Britney Spears album)|Glory]]'' (2016). By the late 2010s, she had earned more than two dozen US [[Top 40]] hits, including two more US number-ones, "[[3 (Britney Spears song)|3]]" and "[[Hold It Against Me]]". In 2019, Spears withdrew from regular concert touring to focus on her [[Britney Spears conservatorship case|conservatorship case]], sparking the #FreeBritney movement and the documentary ''[[Framing Britney Spears]]''. In 2021, she publicly testified against her family and management for maltreatment, which lead to the dissolution of the conservatorship. Her 2023 memoir, ''[[The Woman in Me (memoir)|The Woman in Me]]'', debuted atop [[The New York Times Best Seller list|''The New York Times'' Best Seller]] list.

With over 150 million records sold worldwide, Spears is one of the [[List of best-selling music artists|best-selling music artists]] in history, with [[Nielsen SoundScan]] ranking her the fourth best-selling female album artist of their era. ''[[Forbes]]'' listed her as the world's [[Forbes list of the world's highest-paid musicians#Female|highest-paid female musician]] twice, in 2000 and 2012. Spears has received [[List of awards and nominations received by Britney Spears|numerous industry awards]], including one [[Grammy Award]], six [[MTV Video Music Awards]], and seven [[Billboard Music Award|''Billboard'' Music Awards]] (including the [[Billboard Millennium Award|Millennium Award]]). Outside of music, Spears has launched [[Britney Spears products|numerous products]] over the years, with her 2005 fragrance [[Fantasy (fragrance)|Fantasy]] with [[Elizabeth Arden, Inc.]] generating over $1.5 billion in sales by 2011.
